Extracted team-abbreviation normalization into a new zero-dependency leaf package internal/teams (mirroring internal/positions), since fantrax couldn't import projections (cycle) to reuse NormalizeTeam directly as the brief assumed. Applied at all 5 raw-upstream construction sites in internal/fantrax (client.go x3, daily_fpts.go, gs_check.go — 2 more than the issue's estimate, found via a full grep of MLBTeam: assignments), and repointed schedule/prospects callers at the new package. Regression test proves toPlayer normalizes non-canonical abbreviations (SDP, OAK) at construction.

Verified live against the 2026 season (GetScoringPeriodsAndTeams + GetAllMatchupEntries cross-check): period 16's merged All-Star-break span (Jul13-26) is uniform — every MatchupEntry in that window reports ScoringPeriod=16, and each distinct Date maps to exactly one period, confirming the acceptance criteria's open question. Fixed cmd/optimize.go's GS-budget block to call ft.GetScoringPeriodsAndTeams() + fantrax.FindCurrentPeriod(periods, today) — the literal same call+helper gscheck.go already uses (FindJustEndedPeriod, same package/data source), rather than GetAllMatchupEntries as the issue's suggested fix proposed. This is a stronger guarantee than 'structurally similar': both call sites now share one derivation, not two independently-implemented ones that happen to agree. Old GetMatchupWeekNumberForDate/matchupWeekRanges untouched — still used by recap.go for week-ordinal display and by this same function for weekStart/weekEnd bounds, both out of scope. New regression test locks in the merged-period date-range shape.

Added an in-flight check to POST /v1/jobs/{name} (internal/lineupapi/handler.go handleJob + jobs.go inFlightRun): before launching, scans recent runs for a RUNNING entry whose command's first token matches the requested job name, and rejects with 409 if found. Matches on base job name (ignoring flags) per the acceptance criteria's 'same job name' wording, since the race (e.g. claims cursor) isn't specific to one param combination. Bounded by a 2h staleness window since entrypoint.sh only flips RUNNING->SUCCESS/FAILED after the command exits -- a hard crash mid-run would otherwise leave a ledger entry RUNNING forever and permanently block future manual triggers for that job, which would have been a worse regression than the race it fixes. Fails open (nil RunStore or a List error skips the check) since this is a race-reduction guard, not a strict lock, and local  runs without Runs configured at all. 8 new tests covering the pure inFlightRun logic (name matching, staleness, fail-open paths) plus full handler-level 409/202 wiring.

Added a LifecycleRule to StateBucket (infra/infra.go) scoped to the cache/ prefix, expiring noncurrent versions after 14 days (top of the suggested 7-14d range). Verified via cdk diff -c enableBuild=true against live AWS: the only resource change is the new LifecycleConfiguration on StateBucket (plus an expected Lambda code-hash bump from the unrelated rosterbot-744 fix already on this branch) -- no other resources touched, confirming analysis/, backtest/, runs/, lineup/, session/, and claims/ prefixes are unaffected. NOT deployed -- cdk deploy is a real infra change and needs explicit go-ahead. Incidentally found and fixed pre-existing dependency drift in lambda/go.mod (root module had a dependabot bump that was never re-tidied into the lambda submodule) which was blocking cdk diff's asset bundling; unrelated to this issue but needed to unblock verification.

Fixed: estimated future GS demand now weighted by fractional share (not ceil-rounded to a full-value placeholder), and a 10% certainty margin extends the exact-tie today-preference rule to near-ties. Confirmed future starts unaffected. 3 new regression tests.

Lowered non-starter SP discount from 0.10x to 0.05x (top of suggested 3-5% range), extracted as exported optimizer.NonStarterSPDiscount so backtest.go's mirrored discount can't drift independently (was a duplicated magic number). Regression test proves a 20pt/G non-starting SP no longer outranks a modest 1.1pt/G real RP. Note: did not build a full old-vs-new forward-planning replay harness (would require point-in-time projection snapshots this repo doesn't capture for what-if comparisons) — verified via unit regression test + full suite, not a live historical backtest replay.

Hitters switched to bounded 30-day window (validated: beats YTD ~1 pt/game/day over full season + 8wk); pitchers unchanged (recency immaterial to pitcher lineups). Implemented, tested, verified live; docs updated.
